cot_prefix: Okay, let me read through the passage and the question carefully. The question is asking about the findings of the World Bank study in 1974 regarding economic growth in some backward countries. The options are about who benefited from the growth. Looking at the passage, the relevant part is: "A major study by the World Bank in 1974 concluded that it is now clear that more than decades of rapid growth in developing countries has been of little benefit to a third of their population." Wait, the wording here is a bit tricky. The study says that "more than decades of rapid growth... has been of little benefit to a third of their population." So, the growth brought little benefit to one-third, meaning that two-thirds did not experience this little benefit? Or does it mean that the benefit was limited such that a third of the population received almost nothing? The next sentence says, "The World Bank study showed that increase in GNP per capita did not promise important
